dairy-free-pumpkin-bread.name: Dairy-free Pumpkin Bread
instructions: Heat oven to 177°C. Grease 9x5-inch loaf pan. Line bottom with waxed paper or parchment; grease paper.

In large bowl, mix flour, sugar, baking soda, salt, cinnamon, ginger, and nutmeg with electric mixer on low speed. Add remaining ingredients. Beat on low speed until ingredients are moistened. Beat on medium speed just until creamy. Spread evenly in pan.

Bake 60 to 70 minutes or until toothpick inserted in center comes out clean. Cool in pan 10 minutes. Remove from pan and cool completely on cooling rack, about 1 hour. Store in refrigerator if desired.

instructions: Inicia la receta, toma el cambur o la banana y corta en rodajas. Reserva.
Take your pineapple and cut a slice or several so that you can have the half and half cup that you need for the shake.
Then cut pineapple into cubes so that it can be well processed in the liquefied.
That's half the datas.
In a blender adds the banana cut into slices, pineapples, lemon juice and dates. Then pour the vegetable milk.
Ahora, procesa muy bien todos los ingredientes hasta que se forme un batido bien cremoso y sin grumos.
Serve in a glass, you can go with some ice and a pitcher.
Enjoy!

delicious-coffee-and-cream-cheese-telecu.name: Delicious Coffee And Cream Cheese
instructions: Drain the gelatin leaves in cold water for 5 minutes. Crush 50 g of cookies and book. Pass the curds through the hammock, add the milk and sugar and beat well.

In a glass, beat the clears into a firm castle. Drain the gelatin leaves and melt them in the microwave without letting them boil. Incorporating them into the preparation of the curds, always knocking, and then wrap this prepared with the clear ones.
In a bowl, have alternate layers of cream and biscuits, previously passed by coffee, and then take them to the refrigerator. Remove, sprinkle with crushed biscuit and serve.

delicious-orange-bundt-cake-comedera.name: Delicious Orange Bundt Cake
instructions: For this recipe I like to use a mixer as it's more useful and will give you more spontaneity. But if you don't have the equipment, there's no problem. You can do this manually.
In a bowl, you'll add butter and sugar and we'll beat at an average speed for 5 minutes. When we put these two ingredients together, we will get a whiteness and very creamy mixture.
Slow down your mixer and you will add the eggs, one by one progressively until they have all been integrated.
Then you'll add the juice and the orange frying. Mixes up and incorporates.
Mix wheat flour, cinnamon, pint of salt and bake powder and sig them.
Ahora agregaremos de a poco la mitad de la harina de trigo, mezcla muy bien.
We stop the mixer and we will use a miser or pat to incorporate the rest of the flour and with wrapped movements we will mix. Be sure there aren't any clumps.
Preheat your oven at 170 degrees Celsius.
Fold your boldt with some butter. Stretch the mixture into the mold and give some noodle so that there's no air at the bottom and everything's settled.

Hornea for about 45 minutes, at this time should be super ready. But, apply the technique of inserting a wooden stick, if you get out humerus, you'll leave it 5 minutes more, if it gets completely dry, it's more than perfect.
We're done! Let cool so that you can crumble and taste.
Enjoy!

delicious-puff-pastry-with-pastry-cream-.name: Delicious Puff Pastry With Pastry Cream
delicious-puff-pastry-with-pastry-cream-.original_name: Delícia folhada com creme de pasteleiro
instructions: Light a pan with 200 ml of milk and let it warm well. In a bowl, mix the yolks well with the egg and sugar, add the flour and the remaining milk and stir. Then add the warm milk, in yarn and always stirring, lay back to the pot and bring it to the fire, stirring continuously until thicken. Remove from the fire, lie in a bowl and let it cool.

Start the oven at 200oC and pass an oven tray through cold water. Stretch slightly the leafy mass with the roll over a floured surface and cut it into small rectangles (about 6x10 cm). Then pick them with a fork and arrange them on the oven tray.

Take it in the oven for 15 minutes, then reduce the temperature to 170oC and leave another 10 minutes. Remove, let it cool, bar the sides with the pastry cream, sprinkle with powdered sugar and serve.

demi-glace.name: Demi-glace
instructions: Preheat the oven to 232°C (230 degrees C). Place veal bones in a roasting pan.
Roast bones in the preheated oven until well-browned, about 1 hour 15 minutes.
While the bones are roasting, drizzle oil onto a baking sheet. Add onions, carrots, and celery, then spread tomato paste over top. Mix until vegetables are well coated.
Roast vegetables on another rack in the oven until well-browned, about 45 minutes.
Transfer vegetables and bones to a large stockpot. Pour 10 quarts water into the stockpot and set over medium-high heat.

Meanwhile, pour 2 cups water into the roasting pan used for the bones. Set the pan on the stovetop over high heat; bring liquid to a boil while scraping the browned bits of food off of the bottom of the pan with a wooden spoon.
Pour liquid and browned bits into the stockpot.

Bring contents of the stockpot to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer gently, skimming foam as necessary, until meat and all connective tissues are completely off the bones, about 18 hours.
Set a colander over a bowl. Ladle vegetables, bones, and meat into the colander. Discard vegetables, bones, and meat; return any broth to the stockpot.
Bring broth to a boil. Boil until broth reduces to approximately 1 gallon in volume, 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Strain broth through a fine-mesh strainer into a large container set in an ice bath; chill to room temperature, 30 to 45 minutes.
Cover the container with a lid or plastic wrap, then chill in the refrigerator until demi-glace is cold and set, 8 hours to overnight.
Scrape and discard any fat from the surface.
Turn demi-glace out onto a work surface. Cut into 16 blocks. Wrap each block in plastic wrap and store in a resealable plastic bag in the freezer.
Enjoy!

detox-smoothie-step-by-step-comedera.name: Detox Smoothy: step by step
instructions: Add all the ingredients to the blister and moan at maximum speed.
Wait till the ingredients are fully incorporated and have a smooth texture.
Serves right away and enjoys.

double-baked-buttery-shortbread-cookies.name: Double-baked Buttery Shortbread Cookies

ingredients.prep: preferably European-style high-fat butter such as Plugra

ingredients.item: Finely sliced preserved yuzu or lemon peel or fresh lemon zest

instructions: Whisk all-purpose flour, cornstarch, and rice flour in a medium bowl to combine. Using a bench scraper or pastry blender (or your fingers if you don’t have either), work butter, sugar, and salt in a large bowl until butter is cut into smallish pieces and sugar is no longer visible. Add dry ingredients to butter mixture and cut into butter until a rough dough starts to form with some loose flour visible. Turn out onto a clean surface and, working with small handfuls at a time, smear dough across surface with the heel of your hand to work butter and flour together. Continue to work until all the dough is blended. Press into a rectangle, being careful not to overwork dough; you want it to just hold together. Wrap tightly in plastic and chill until firm, about 30 minutes.

Preheat oven to 325°. Roll out dough between 2 sheets of parchment paper to about a 10x8" rectangle ⅓"–½" thick. Turn over a large baking sheet and place dough on top. Remove top sheet of parchment; bake shortbread, rotating baking sheet once, until pale but not golden and nearly firm to the touch, 25–35 minutes. Remove shortbread from oven and cut into 3x1¼" bars, separating them slightly once cut. Bake, rotating baking sheet once, until deep golden brown, nutty-smelling, and very crisp, 25–35 minutes longer. Let cool.
Just before serving, dust shortbread with powdered sugar and top with cacao nibs and preserved lemon peel.
Do Ahead: Shortbreads can be baked 3 days ahead. Store tightly wrapped at room temperature.

dried-raisin-argiro.name: Dried Raisin
instructions: For traditional dried raisins we will need: sheet metal, grill, tulpan.
We choose grabes ripe but strong. We leave the bunches as they are, whole.
Carefully Wash them and let them draw well. If the punch is too nick, cut it into smaller pieces for faster drying.
Put the grill on top of the sheet and touch the bunches, not too close to air and melt primarily.
Cover them with tulip and leave them in strong sun for several days. Sometimes, we flip the bunches to dry and dry well.
To make sure the raisin is ready, we gently shake the punch and if the raisin falls easily then it is ready.
We chew carefuly all the clusters and keep the raisin in glass sterile jars, placing intermediary leaves. Attention, the raisin must dry well because the brightest animation will make us mold.
We can also dry it, towards the end, in the evening, at low temperature for more safety. When ready, keep the raisin in a dark and cool campnet.
